question: How do I create an account?
answer: Tap "Sign Up" on the login screen, enter your email and password, then check your email for a confirmation link. Once confirmed, log in and complete the onboarding questionnaire so IronZ can personalize your plan and nutrition targets.

question: Do I need to complete the onboarding questionnaire?
answer: Yes. The onboarding questionnaire collects your fitness level, goals, available equipment, and training preferences. IronZ uses this information to generate personalized training plans, nutrition targets, and hydration goals. Without it, the app cannot tailor recommendations to you.

question: Can I use IronZ on multiple devices?
answer: Yes. All your data syncs to the cloud. Log in with the same account on any device and your profile, workouts, nutrition, and hydration data will be there.

question: I forgot my password. How do I reset it?
answer: Tap "Forgot Password" on the login screen. Enter your email and you will receive a reset link. Click the link, set a new password, and log back in.

question: How does IronZ generate my training plan?
answer: IronZ uses a rules-based philosophy engine. When you add a race, the plan is built backwards from race day using phase ratios defined in the training philosophy (Base / Build / Peak / Taper), with phase weeks scaling to the time you have available. Your Training Inputs — which days you train, long day preference, session length, sport mix — feed the weekly template. Session types and loads modulate by phase so Base is aerobic-focused, Build adds intensity, Peak is race-pace work, and Taper cuts volume. Plans are generated deterministically without AI. AI is only used for Ask IronZ freeform questions.

question: How do I add a workout to my calendar?
answer: Tap the "+ Quick Add" button on the home screen. Choose the workout type (Running, Cycling, Swimming, Strength, HIIT, Yoga, etc.), set duration and intensity, and save. The session lands on the selected date and counts toward your weekly totals.

question: Can I edit a workout after it's been generated?
answer: Yes. Tap "Edit" on any workout card to modify exercises, sets, reps, weights, or duration. You can swap exercises, add new ones, or remove ones you do not want. The change sticks only to that day — the rest of your plan stays intact.

question: What are training zones and how are they calculated?
answer: Training zones are pace or heart rate ranges that target specific physiological adaptations. IronZ calculates running zones using the Jack Daniels VDOT model based on your race times or time trial results. Zones include Recovery (Z1), Easy (Z2), Tempo (Z3), Threshold (Z4), and Speed (Z5). There are intentional gaps between some zones — these represent paces that do not serve a specific training purpose.

question: Why are there gaps between my running zones?
answer: The gaps are intentional and scientifically correct. In the Jack Daniels model, Easy pace and Tempo pace do not connect directly. The space between them is sometimes called "no man's land" — too fast to be easy, too slow to produce tempo adaptations. Training in this range is less effective than staying in a defined zone.

question: How often should I update my training zones?
answer: Update your zones whenever you run a new race or time trial that reflects your current fitness. A good rule of thumb is every 4-8 weeks during a training cycle, or after a breakthrough performance.

question: Can I ask IronZ to change my plan?
answer: Yes. Use the "Ask IronZ" input on the home screen to tell IronZ what's going on — "my knee is sore" or "traveling Thursday-Sunday" — and it will propose a schedule adjustment (reduce intensity or remove a session) for you to confirm before anything is applied. For direct exercise edits, tap "Edit" on the workout card.

question: How does carb loading work in IronZ?
answer: In the final three days before an endurance race (marathon, half marathon, Olympic+ triathlon, century, gran fondo), your daily carb target ramps from 8 to 9 to 10 g/kg of body weight across T-3 to T-2 to T-1. Protein stays at training levels; fat drops to roughly 20% of calories to make room for carbs. The meal plan picks carb-forward recipes (oats, pancakes, pasta, rice bowls) and a "Carb Load · T-N · X g/kg" badge appears on the meal plan so you know why the targets shifted.

question: Can I sync Strava with IronZ?
answer: Yes. Go to Settings → Integrations → Strava and tap Connect. You'll be redirected to Strava to authorize, then sent back to IronZ. Once connected, your completed Strava activities (runs, rides, swims, strength) appear on your IronZ calendar alongside manually-logged workouts. Authorization goes through Strava's OAuth flow — IronZ never sees your Strava password.

question: How does IronZ calculate my nutrition targets?
answer: Daily calories are computed from your body weight, height, age, and gender using the Mifflin-St Jeor BMR equation, then multiplied by an activity factor that shifts with the day's training load (rest, easy, moderate, hard, long, race). Protein is held at ~0.9 g/lb (athletic level). Fat defaults to ~28% of calories and carbs fill the remainder — except on carb-load days, where carbs ramp to 8-10 g/kg and fat drops to ~20%. You can override any macro with the slider on the nutrition card; the override persists for that day.

question: Can I log meals and track macros?
answer: Yes. Tap "Log a Meal" under Nutrition to add food by name, barcode scan, or photo. Calories, protein, carbs, and fat are counted against your daily target. You can also set food preferences, dietary restrictions, and allergies so the suggested meal plan respects them.

question: Is the nutrition advice a replacement for a dietitian?
answer: No. IronZ provides general nutrition guidance for athletic performance based on published research. It is not medical nutrition therapy. If you have specific dietary needs, food allergies, metabolic conditions, or eating disorders, consult a registered dietitian.

question: How does hydration tracking work?
answer: Tap "My Bottle" to quickly log your default bottle size (set in settings), or tap "Quick Add" to choose a different size (8oz, 12oz, 16oz, 20oz, etc.). Your daily total is tracked against a personalized hydration target based on your body weight and activity level.

question: Can I log hydration for a previous day?
answer: Yes. Navigate to the previous day using the date selector and add hydration entries retroactively.

question: How is my daily hydration target calculated?
answer: Your hydration target is based on your body weight (a common starting point is half your body weight in ounces), adjusted for training intensity, climate, and activity duration. The philosophy engine refines this based on your profile.

question: What is Ask IronZ?
answer: Ask IronZ is the input at the top of your home screen where you can ask training or nutrition questions, or tell IronZ what's going on ("my back is sore", "traveling all week"). FAQ questions return a direct documented answer; coaching questions and state changes are handled by an AI assistant constrained by the IronZ training philosophy so responses stay consistent with our evidence-based approach.

question: Is Ask IronZ a real coach?
answer: No. Ask IronZ is an AI assistant, not a certified personal trainer or licensed professional. It provides guidance based on the IronZ training philosophy and published exercise science. For medical concerns or individualized coaching, consult a qualified professional.

question: Can Ask IronZ change my schedule?
answer: Yes — for schedule adjustments. Tell IronZ something like "I hurt my knee" or "I'm traveling Thursday to Sunday" and it will propose a restriction (reduce intensity or remove sessions) for you to confirm before anything is applied. For editing exercises inside a single workout (swap squats for lunges, change weights), use the "Edit" button on the workout card directly.

question: Is IronZ free?
answer: Core features are available for free. Premium features (advanced AI coaching, detailed analytics, and integrations) may require a subscription. See the Pricing page for current options.

question: How do I cancel my subscription?
answer: You can cancel anytime through the Apple App Store or Google Play Store subscription settings, or through your account settings in the app. Your access continues until the end of the current billing period.

question: How do I delete my account and data?
answer: In the app: go to Settings → Delete Account and confirm. This permanently removes your profile, workouts, meals, race events, and plans. If you can't sign in to trigger the in-app flow, email ironzsupport@gmail.com and we'll delete the account within 30 days of the verified request, as described in our Privacy Policy.

question: Does IronZ connect to Strava?
answer: Yes. In Settings → Integrations, tap Connect Strava. After authorizing through Strava's OAuth flow you'll be redirected back to IronZ, and your completed runs, rides, swims, and strength sessions will start appearing on your calendar alongside manually-logged workouts.

question: Will IronZ support Garmin or Whoop?
answer: Garmin and Whoop integrations are on the roadmap. These will allow IronZ to incorporate heart rate, recovery, and sleep data into your training recommendations.

question: My data is not showing up on a new device.
answer: Make sure you are logged in with the same account. Your data syncs to the cloud automatically. If data is still missing, log out and log back in to trigger a sync. If the issue persists, email ironzsupport@gmail.com.

question: The app is stuck on "Building your workout."
answer: Close and reopen the app. If the issue persists, check your internet connection — plan generation runs locally but cloud sync and Ask IronZ require a connection. If you're on the web, clear your browser cache and reload. If still stuck, email ironzsupport@gmail.com with a screenshot.

question: I found a bug. How do I report it?
answer: Email ironzsupport@gmail.com with a description of what happened, what you expected, and a screenshot if possible. Include your device type and browser/app version.
